introSection: 88 Train (Korean: 88얄차) was a sit-down roller coaster built by Japanese firm Meisho Amusement Machines previously located at Children's Grand Park in Songdong-Gu, Seoul, South Korea.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened in 1984 and operated until 2012 or earlier. It was taken down by January 2013 and was replaced by Family Coaster.

One of the ride's cars remains on display at the park.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: The Korean name is "88열차" (88 Train) and the English Name is "Loop and Corkscrew Jet Coaster".

sections.title: Design
sections.content: 88 Train had three inversions - a vertical loop and a double-corkscrew.

fullText:
88 Train (Korean: 88얄차) was a sit-down roller coaster built by Japanese firm Meisho Amusement Machines previously located at Children's Grand Park in Songdong-Gu, Seoul, South Korea.

The ride opened in 1984 and operated until 2012 or earlier. It was taken down by January 2013 and was replaced by Family Coaster.

One of the ride's cars remains on display at the park.

The Korean name is "88열차" (88 Train) and the English Name is "Loop and Corkscrew Jet Coaster".

88 Train had three inversions - a vertical loop and a double-corkscrew.
